sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
So the charcoal shape moved from cols 46-49 to cols 50-53 (shifted right by 4 columns), and the yellow strip moved from cols 50-51 to cols 54-55 (also shifted right by 4 columns). The G (dark gray) area expanded to the right as well.
It seems like clicking on the white area causes the dark gray area to expand and pushes objects to the right. The white area is being consumed.
Maybe the goal is to push the charcoal shape to overlap with the yellow strip, or to push it to a specific position. Currently:
- Charcoal shape: cols 50-53
- Yellow strip: cols 54-55
They're adjacent! The charcoal shape is right next to the yellow strip. Maybe the goal is to make them overlap or to align them in some way.
Wait, let me look at the initial positions again:
- Initial charcoal: cols 46-49 (centered around col 47-48)
- Initial yellow: cols 50-51
- They were already adjacent initially!
After the move:
- Charcoal: cols 50-53
- Yellow: cols 54-55
- Still adjacent, both shifted right by 4.
So clicking on the white area shifts everything to the right. Maybe the goal is to shift them until the charcoal shape reaches the blue blocks or some other target.
Let me look at where the blue blocks are:
- Blue blocks: cols 60-63
If I keep pushing right, eventually the charcoal shape and yellow strip will reach the blue blocks. Maybe the goal is to make them collide with the blue blocks.
Let me try clicking on the white area again to push more.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Clicking on white background shifts the charcoal shape and yellow strip to the right. The dark gray area expands. Goal likely involves moving these objects to a target position. Currently charcoal at cols 50-53, yellow at cols 54-55, blue blocks at cols 60-63.
Plan: Click on white area again to push objects further right toward the blue blocks.
